feature: Inventory and dependency graph: HTTP availability check / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on HTTP availability check cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for HTTP availability check: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover HTTP availability check, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for HTTP availability check.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for HTTP availability check.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Digital Experience and Synthetic Checks HTTP availability check inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: http-availability-check_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for HTTP availability check cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for HTTP availability check; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for HTTP availability check.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for HTTP availability check, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for HTTP availability check.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for HTTP availability check.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing HTTP availability check data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling HTTP availability check inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for HTTP availability check.
acceptance_criteria: Given HTTP availability check data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: HTTP availability check connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for user journey health: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Support read, validate, mutate, rollback, and audit workflows for user journey health with cost guardrails.
backend_scope: Add validated commands, dry-run support, RBAC checks, and audit records for user journey health.
frontend_scope: Add action menu, confirmations, diff previews, and result status for user journey health.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Digital Experience and Synthetic Checks user journey health operations with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: user-journey-health_cost_operations evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for user journey health cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for user journey health; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for user journey health.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for user journey health, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for user journey health.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for user journey health.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing user journey health data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling user journey health operations broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for user journey health.
acceptance_criteria: Given user journey health data exists, when the cost operations workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: user journey health connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
